message:
test: hermetic env, accept-risk e2e, and golden updates for hardened output

== tests/agent_profile_comprehensive.rs: hermetic environment ==

Trust-level tests previously inherited `HOME`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, and
`TMPDIR` from the developer's shell, so `dcg` would read the real
`~/.config/dcg/` and write to the real history database during the
test run — flaky failures in CI (different config layouts), and a
risk of accidentally polluting the developer's allowlist or escalation
counters with test commands like `rm -rf /`.

Adds:
- `make_isolated_home()` returns a fresh `tempfile::TempDir` with a
  pre-created `.config/dcg` subdir; dropped at end of test.
- `apply_hermetic_env()` calls `cmd.env_clear()` and re-exports only
  `PATH` plus the isolated `HOME` / `TMPDIR` / `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, plus
  `NO_COLOR=1` so denial output stays plain in test logs.
- `dcg_binary()` now prefers `option_env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_dcg")` (set by
  Cargo when building integration tests against a binary target) and
  falls back to `target/<profile>/dcg` derived from `current_exe()`.
- Both `run_hook_mode_with_env` and `run_robot_mode_with_env` apply
  hermetic env BEFORE per-test env vars, so callers can still override.

== tests/suggest_allowlist_e2e.rs: --accept-risk gate coverage ==

New end-to-end tests covering the `--accept-risk` flag added in
`src/cli.rs`:
- Verifies `--apply` skips `RequireConfirmation` suggestions (e.g.
  patterns touching system paths) without `--accept-risk`, including
  a stable-id check on the audit-log row that records the rejection.
- Verifies `--apply --accept-risk` writes those same suggestions to
  the active layer.
- Verifies `NeverSuggest` entries (e.g. `kubectl delete`,
  `terraform destroy`) remain unwritable regardless of `--accept-risk`
  — those are filtered upstream in `filter_suggestions_for_safety`
  and must never reach `apply_suggestions_by_index`.

== tests/golden_artifacts.rs + tests/golden/artifacts/explain/*.json ==

Three golden fixtures — `destructive_git_reset.json`,
`heredoc_python_rmtree.json`, `safe_git_status.json` — drop trailing
fields whose values are now elided by the hardened ANSI stripper and
escalation-copy changes:
- `destructive_git_reset.json` no longer carries the empty
  copy-paste hint that was being absorbed by the broken
  `strip_ansi_codes` (the OSC hyperlink in the hint terminated on
  the wrong byte and ate everything after).
- `heredoc_python_rmtree.json` and `safe_git_status.json` similarly
  drop fields that the previous ANSI stripper was silently consuming.
- `tests/golden_artifacts.rs` updates the matching record-shape
  assertions and the `record_count` expectation for each fixture.

The golden artifacts are regenerated against the hardened output
path; behavior under the previous (broken) path was incidental and
not user-facing — no functional change for downstream consumers of
the `--explain --json` output.
